dichotomies.name: Extraversion / Introversion
dichotomies.dimension: Where you direct attention and draw energy

E.label: Extraversion
E.description: Oriented toward the outer world of people, activity, and things; gains energy from interaction and tends to act, then reflect.

I.label: Introversion
I.description: Oriented toward the inner world of ideas and reflection; gains energy from solitude and tends to reflect, then act.

S.label: Sensing
S.description: Trusts concrete, tangible, present-focused information gathered directly through the five senses; attends to facts and details.

N.label: Intuition
N.description: Trusts patterns, meanings, connections, and future possibilities; reads between the lines and focuses on the big picture. Abbreviated N to distinguish it from Introversion.

T.label: Thinking
T.description: Decides from a detached, objective standpoint using logic, consistency, and cause-and-effect analysis.

F.label: Feeling
F.description: Decides by weighing personal and shared values and the impact of choices on people, seeking empathy and harmony.

dichotomies.name: Judging / Perceiving
dichotomies.dimension: How you orient to the outer world

J.label: Judging
J.description: Prefers structure, planning, and closure; likes matters decided and settled. (Indicates an extraverted judging function is used in the outer world, not that the person is judgmental.)

P.label: Perceiving
P.description: Prefers flexibility, spontaneity, and openness; likes to keep options open and gather more information. (Indicates an extraverted perceiving function is used in the outer world.)
